decisions.decision: Replace scripts/context/ entirely — delete old module, build new universal engine in the same location
decisions.rationale: No adapter overhead, no dual-module maintenance. Clean cut.

decisions.decision: tree-sitter (py-tree-sitter + per-language grammars) replaces stdlib ast
decisions.rationale: Proven multi-language AST parsing. Axon validates this approach. Supports 50+ languages via grammar packages.

decisions.decision: KuzuDB replaces SQLite — graph-native storage with Cypher query support
decisions.rationale: Native graph operations (traversals, path queries) instead of SQL joins on adjacency tables. Better performance for relationship-heavy queries.

decisions.decision: Leiden algorithm via igraph + leidenalg replaces label propagation
decisions.rationale: Leiden produces higher-quality community partitions than label propagation, especially on larger graphs.

decisions.decision: Hybrid search: BM25 + semantic vectors (384-dim) + fuzzy matching
decisions.rationale: BM25 alone misses semantic similarity. Vector search catches conceptually related code. Fuzzy handles typos and partial names.
Small, fast model suitable for code search. 384-dim keeps storage reasonable.

decisions.decision: watchfiles for live re-indexing on file changes
decisions.rationale: Keeps graph in sync without manual graph-index commands. watchfiles uses native OS events (inotify/FSEvents).

decisions.decision: Jaccard similarity + git history change coupling
decisions.rationale: Git coupling captures temporal co-change patterns that structural analysis misses.
Separate requirements file keeps graph deps isolated. Standard pip workflow.

decisions.decision: Stdlib-only constraint removed for the graph module
decisions.rationale: Tree-sitter, KuzuDB, igraph, sentence-transformers require external packages. Graph is an advanced feature that justifies deps.

decisions.decision: CLI subcommands + Python API only. No MCP server.
decisions.rationale: cnogo runs inline — no daemons, no servers. Graph accessed via existing workflow_memory.py subcommands and Python imports.

decisions.decision: .cnogo/graph.kuzu/ for KuzuDB, ~/.cache/cnogo/models/ for embedding model cache
decisions.rationale: Graph DB is project-specific (gitignored). Embedding model is shared across projects to avoid re-downloading.

constraints: Old scripts/context/ is deleted entirely — no adapter, no dual-module period
All 14 existing analysis phases must be re-implemented with new deps
Existing CLI subcommands (graph-index, graph-enrich, graph-suggest-scope, etc.) must keep working
Existing workflow.py integration functions must keep working
install.sh must handle pip install of graph dependencies
Embedding model cached at ~/.cache/cnogo/models/ (shared across projects)
Tree-sitter grammars installed via pip (tree-sitter-python, tree-sitter-typescript, etc.)
Python 3.11+ may be required (verify tree-sitter and KuzuDB minimum versions)
openQuestions: Which tree-sitter grammar packages to include in requirements-graph.txt for initial release?
Should watchfiles run as a background subprocess or integrate into graph-index --watch?
What is the minimum Python version for all new deps (3.10 vs 3.11 vs 3.12)?

research.findings: Graph-powered code intelligence engine with nearly identical architecture. Uses tree-sitter, KuzuDB, Leiden, semantic embeddings. Validates the multi-language graph approach.
